content:
# 2 Corinthians Chapter 6 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Codex Sinaiticus (א), 4th century CE | **Source Image:** source-texts/greek-NT/2CO_006_page_001.jpg

1. And working together [with him] we also urge you not to receive the grace of God in vain.

2. For he says, "In an acceptable time I listened to you, and in a day of salvation I helped you." Behold, now [is] the acceptable time; behold, now [is] the day of salvation.

3. Giving no cause of stumbling in anything, that the ministry may not be blamed,

4. but in everything commending ourselves as ministers of God: in much endurance, in afflictions, in necessities, in distresses,

5. in beatings, in imprisonments, in tumults, in labors, in sleeplessness, in fastings,

6. in purity, in knowledge, in patience, in kindness, in [the] Holy Spirit, in love unfeigned,

7. in [the] word of truth, in [the] power of God, through the weapons of righteousness on the right and on the left,

8. through glory and dishonor, through evil report and good report; as deceivers and [yet] true,

9. as unknown and [yet] well-known, as dying and behold we live, as being disciplined and [yet] not put to death,

10. as grieving [yet] always rejoicing, as poor [yet] making many rich, as having nothing and [yet] possessing all [things].

11. The mouth of us has opened to you, Korinthians; the heart of us has been enlarged.

12. You are not restricted by us, but you are restricted in the affections of you.

13. And [as] the same return — I speak as to children — you also be enlarged.

14. Do not become unequally yoked with unbelievers. For what partnership [has] righteousness and lawlessness? And what fellowship [has] light with darkness?

15. And what agreement [has] Christos with Beliar? Or what portion [has] a believer with an unbeliever?

16. And what agreement [has] the temple of God with idols? For we are [the] temple of [the] living God, just as God said, "I will dwell in them and walk among [them], and I will be their God, and they will be my people."

17. Therefore "come out from the midst of them and be separated," says [the] Lord, "and do not touch [the] unclean [thing], and I will receive you."

18. "And I will be a father to you, and you will be sons and daughters to me," says [the] Lord Almighty.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

1. **v.2 — Isaiah 49:8:** Paul quotes the LXX of Isaiah's Servant Song, applying the "acceptable time" to the present moment of gospel proclamation.

2. **vv.4-10 — The hardship catalogue:** The most extensive peristasis (hardship list) in Paul. Three triads of affliction (vv.4-5), nine qualities of character (vv.6-7), and seven antithetical pairs (vv.8-10). The structure is carefully crafted.

3. **v.15 — "Beliar":** Βελιάρ — a name for Satan found in Jewish apocalyptic literature (Jubilees, Testament of the Twelve Patriarchs). Its appearance here shows Paul's familiarity with intertestamental tradition.

4. **vv.16-18 — Catena of OT quotations:** Paul assembles a chain of texts: Lev 26:12 / Ezek 37:27 (v.16), Isa 52:11 / Ezek 20:34 (v.17), 2 Sam 7:14 / Isa 43:6 (v.18). The "sons and daughters" addition is not found in any single OT text — Paul adapts the tradition.

5. **v.18 — "sons and daughters":** The addition of "daughters" (θυγατέρας) to the 2 Samuel 7:14 quotation is significant — Paul expands the Davidic covenant promise to include women explicitly.

— EMET, AI Translator
